title: Problem-Solving — Breaking Big Problems Down (Mid)
key_insight: Every complex problem is just a collection of small problems. The skill is knowing how to break it apart.
body:
## The Problem-Solving Process

```
1. UNDERSTAND the problem before writing code
   → What exactly is the input?
   → What exactly should the output be?

2. DECOMPOSE into smaller pieces
   → What are the independent sub-tasks?

3. SOLVE each piece
   → Start with the simplest first
   → Test each piece independently

4. COMBINE the pieces
   → Connect them together
   → Test the whole thing
```

## The ML Debugging Checklist

```
When training fails:
□ Is the loss decreasing at all?
□ Is loss = NaN anywhere?
□ Are all tensors on the same device?
□ Is train loss decreasing but val loss plateauing?
□ Did you check the DATA before blaming the model?
```

## Metrics Trap

```python
# 95% accuracy sounds great...
# Until you realise: dataset is 95% non-spam
# So model that always says 'not spam' = 95% accuracy!

# Always ask: is accuracy actually what we care about?
# Sometimes use: precision, recall, F1, AUC instead
```
common_mistakes: Trying to solve everything at once — start with one piece, get it working, then add the next
Writing code before understanding the problem — 5 minutes of thinking saves hours of debugging
Fixing symptoms instead of root causes — if model output is wrong, check data first, then training
mini_exercise: You want to build a semantic search system. Break this into sub-problems (don't write code yet). What data do you need? What algorithm? What interface? Spending time on structure is the most valuable step.
